mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth vs 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the average return in quarters with top-quartile RSAFS growth is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, tight monetary policy dampens economic activity and raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or more than 40% of triggered instances produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and stable or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a goldilocks environment where labor income fuels consumption and low rates support valuations, boosting equity prices over 20 days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is unchanged or declining, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the joint signal rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion phase characterized by low unemployment and rising rates, continued strong industrial production growth signals overheating and impending slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE < 4% and FEDFUNDS > 3% and rising over 6 months)
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, the rank correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is not negative, or the average return when INDPRO growth is above median is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals an imminent recession, causing a sharp negative repricing of equities over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When the UNRATE trigger fires, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or more than 40% of triggered instances yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the month-over-month growth rate of real retail sales exceeds that of industrial production, demand outpaces supply, driving inventory building and corporate profits, which increases equity prices over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth exceeds INDPRO growth, the average 5-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the rank IC of the growth differential < 0.03 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent year-over-year inflation above trend erodes real earnings and forces tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between CPI year-over-year change and 20-day forward returns is not negative (i.e., > 0) or the average return in months when CPI exceeds 3% is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals robust residential investment and economic momentum, but this effect is positive for equity returns only when the monetary environment is accommodative, defined here as the federal funds rate below 3%.
regime_hint: low interest rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 3% and stable)
failure_condition: In regimes with FEDFUNDS below 3%, an increase in HOUST is not followed by positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or the rank correlation between HOUST growth and returns is not positive.
